page: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-16xCPU-32GB is a KVM-based virtual server featuring 16 shared vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 7542 processor running at 2.0 GHz. The system is equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of memory per core. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and has zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks indicate average multi-core capabilities, with stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core scores falling into the middle 50% tier. However, single-core performance is limited, as evidenced by a poor rating in stress-ng single-core tests. Memory bandwidth tests show a discrepancy between cached and uncached operations; small-block cached reads perform at average levels, whereas large-block uncached reads and writes are weak, falling into the bottom 25% tier.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the shared CPU allocation and lack of local storage position this instance as a budget-conscious option for multi-threaded workloads that do not require dedicated CPU cores or high single-core performance. Appropriate workloads include multi-threaded decompression, static web serving, software compilation, and basic database operations, while it is less suitable for memory-intensive or single-threaded tasks.
description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-16xCPU-32GB is a KVM-based virtual server configured with 16 shared AMD EPYC 7542 vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz and 32.0 GB of RAM. Lacking local storage and GPU accelerators, this instance delivers average multi-core performance, as shown by Geekbench and Passmark benchmarks, though its single-core performance and uncached memory bandwidth are weak. The shared CPU allocation makes it a cost-efficient option for multi-threaded workloads such as static web serving, software compilation, and basic database operations that can tolerate shared resource contention.
og_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-16xCPU-32GB is a KVM-based virtual server with 16 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs and 32 GB RAM, delivering average multi-core performance for budget-conscious multi-threaded workloads.
meta_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-16xCPU-32GB is a KVM virtual server with 16 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs and 32 GB RAM, optimized for multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A shared-resource virtual server with sixteen AMD EPYC cores and thirty-two gigabytes of RAM for multi-threaded processing.

32.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB per core
Average multi-core performance in Geekbench and Passmark benchmarks
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Suited for static web serving, software compilation, and multi-threaded decompression
